questions.question: Rf value is less than one because

answers.a: Distance covered by the solute is more than solvent front
answers.b: Distance covered by solvent front is more than distance covered by solute

explanations.a: Incorrect. The solute can never travel farther than the solvent front.
explanations.b: Correct. Rf value is the ratio of distance traveled by the solute to the distance traveled by the solvent front, so it is usually less than 1.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Only the solvent front travels farther than the solute.

questions.question: Rf value is close to 1, then what happened?

answers.c: Separation does not depend on Rf

explanations.a: Incorrect. An Rf value close to 1 generally indicates that the compound has moved with the solvent front, reducing separation quality.
explanations.b: Correct. When Rf is close to 1, the analyte moves nearly with the solvent front, resulting in poor or no separation.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Separation is influenced by Rf values.

explanations.a: Correct but incomplete. Solvent polarity affects Rf value.
explanations.b: Correct but incomplete. Chamber saturation affects Rf value.
explanations.c: Correct but incomplete. pH can affect the movement of analytes.
explanations.d: Correct. Solvent polarity, chamber saturation, and pH all influence Rf values.

questions.question: Solvent chamber should be saturated for

explanations.a: Correct but incomplete. Saturation helps in uniform migration.
explanations.b: Correct but incomplete. Saturation ensures uniform solvent vapor distribution.
explanations.c: Correct. Chamber saturation provides uniform band movement and even solvent vapor distribution.

questions.question: Which loading gas is used in HPTLC?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Hydrogen is not commonly used as a loading gas in HPTLC.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Oxygen is not used as a loading gas.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Helium is mainly used in GC applications.
explanations.d: Correct. Nitrogen (N2) is commonly used as the loading/spraying gas in HPTLC instruments.
